title: Greek Lamb Kebabs
description: A delicious and easy-to-make lamb kebab recipe inspired by Greek cuisine, perfect for a quick dinner or outdoor BBQ.

ingredients.name: Lamb shoulder, cut into 1 1/2-inch cubes
ingredients.notes: Make sure to trim any excess fat

ingredients.name: Extra-virgin olive oil
ingredients.notes: Use a good-quality olive oil for the best flavor

ingredients.name: Fresh oregano leaves
ingredients.notes: Chopped or torn leaves work well

ingredients.name: Garlic cloves, minced
ingredients.notes: Use 1 or 2 cloves depending on your taste

ingredients.name: Salt and pepper
ingredients.notes: Use freshly ground pepper for the best flavor

instructions: Preheat grill to medium-high heat (400°F). Thread lamb cubes onto skewers, leaving a little space between each piece.
In a small bowl, mix together olive oil, oregano, lemon juice, garlic, salt, and pepper. Brush the mixture evenly onto both sides of the lamb cubes.
Grill the kebabs for 8-10 minutes, turning occasionally, or until lamb reaches an internal temperature of 145°F.
Remove the kebabs from the grill and let them rest for 2-3 minutes before serving.
Warm pita bread or Greek flatbread by wrapping it in foil and heating it on the grill for 1-2 minutes.
Serve the kebabs with warm pita bread and your favorite toppings, such as tzatziki sauce or feta cheese.
